MASTER_PAD_VERSION_INFO.MASTER_PAD_INFO: Portable Application Description, or PAD for short, is a data set that is used by shareware authors to disseminate information to anyone interested in their software products. To find out more go to http://pad.asp-software.org

Program_Info.Program_OS_Support: Win11,Win10,Win10 x64,Win8 x64,Win8,Win7 x64,Win7,WinVista,Mac OS X,Mac Other,Linux,Linux Console,Linux Gnome,Linux GPL,Linux Open Source
Program_Info.Program_Change_Info: Adds a portable Windows build (no install; settings kept beside the executable). Includes lossless tree/raw/search/JSONPath/diff/JSONL views, base64 image preview, jump-to-node, format/minify/export and a node detail panel.
Program_Info.Program_System_Requirements: Windows 10/11 (64-bit), macOS 11+, or 64-bit Linux. 4 GB RAM, ~150 MB disk. Edge WebView2 runtime on Windows (preinstalled on Win10/11).

English.Keywords: JSON, JSON viewer, JSON editor, JSONL, NDJSON, JSONPath, JSON diff, JSON formatter, lossless, offline, developer tools
English.Char_Desc_80: Fast, private desktop app to explore, search, query and diff large JSON files.
English.Char_Desc_250: JSON Studio is a private, lossless desktop app for large JSON. Open files up to 500 MB, browse them as a fast tree, search, run JSONPath queries, diff two documents and view JSONL tables - all 100% locally with no uploads, accounts or telemetry.
English.Char_Desc_450: JSON Studio is a private, lossless desktop workspace for large JSON. It opens files up to 500 MB instantly and preserves your data exactly - big integers, high-precision decimals, key order and the original bytes are never altered. Browse a virtualized tree, search keys and values, run RFC 9535 JSONPath queries, structurally diff two documents, view JSONL/NDJSON tables and preview base64 images. Everything runs on your machine: no network, no accounts, no telemetry.
English.Char_Desc_2000:
JSON Studio is a private, lossless workspace for JSON.
Most JSON tools either choke on big files, silently mangle your numbers, or send your data to a server. JSON Studio does none of that. It is a native desktop app built for speed and correctness: open a half-gigabyte file and start navigating immediately, knowing that what you see is byte-for-byte what is on disk. Everything runs on your own machine - no accounts, no uploads, no analytics, no network calls - making it safe for proprietary, regulated or personal data.
What you can do: - Browse huge files smoothly with a virtualized tree and raw view that stay responsive on documents up to 500 MB, with collapse/expand, expand-to-depth, type and item-count badges and full keyboard navigation. - Trust your data: big integers, high-precision decimals, key order and the exact original text are preserved losslessly - nothing is rounded, reordered or reformatted. - Find anything fast: search across keys and values with case sensitivity, whole-field matching, regular expressions and subtree scoping, then jump straight to a match in the tree. - Query with JSONPath: run RFC 9535 expressions with per-document history, execution timing, result counts and export to JSON or JSONL. - Compare two documents with a structural diff (key order ignored) that matches arrays by index or by a key field and highlights what was added, removed or changed. - Work with JSONL / NDJSON as a virtualized table with selectable columns, field statistics and invalid-row highlighting. - Preview embedded images: string values that are data:image URIs or base64 (PNG, JPEG, GIF, WebP, SVG) can be viewed inline. - Format and export: copy pretty-printed or minified JSON, or save the re-serialized document in one click. - Stay in flow with tabs, remembered sessions, live reload when a file changes on disk, a node detail panel with copyable path and value, and System/Light/Dark themes.
A portable Windows build is also available: it runs without installation and keeps its settings beside the executable. JSON Studio is free and open source (MIT) and available for Windows, macOS and Linux.
